docs.title: Home
docs.text:
Retno Dammayatri

GIS & Remote Sensing Analyst

Turning complex geospatial data into clear spatial insight | GIS | Remote Sensing | LiDAR

docs.title: About Me
docs.text:
My journey into GIS began with a fascination for how location and spatial relationships help us understand the world around us. With a background in Geodesy and Geomatics Engineering, I developed a strong foundation in spatial data, remote sensing, and geospatial technologies — combining technical precision with a practical approach to analysing, managing, and visualising geographic information.

I have experience transforming complex spatial datasets into meaningful insights — from designing data collection workflows and managing geospatial databases to developing dashboards, analysing spatial information, and creating clear documentation to support decision-making. I enjoy solving spatial challenges, improving data quality, and building efficient workflows that bridge technical analysis with practical outcomes.

Originally from Indonesia, currently based in Brisbane, Australia on Work and Holiday Visa (subclass 462), I am passionate about applying GIS and remote sensing to spatial intelligence, data management, and geospatial problem-solving. I bring analytical rigour, curiosity, and a commitment to delivering accurate, impactful geospatial solutions.

View My Projects Download CV

docs.title: Skills
docs.text:
• GIS

    • QGIS, ArcGIS Pro, Google Earth Engine
    • Land Use / Land Cover (LULC) Mapping
    • Spatial Analysis & Suitability Mapping
    • Raster & Vector Geoprocessing
    • WebGIS, Cartography & Map Layout Design

  • Remote Sensing

    • Satellite imagery pre-processing (Radiometric Correction, Geometric Correction)
    • Spectral Indiches e.g NDVI, NDBI, NDBaI, EVI etc.
    • Supervised & Unsupervised Classification
    • DSM & DTM Production

  • LiDAR Data Processing

    • Data and Trajectory Extraction
    • Point Cloud Classification / Post Processing
    • Strip Adjustment
    • Map Digitizing and Visualization

  • Photogrammetry / UAV Data Processing

    • Mission planning and flight operations
    • GNSS PPK/Trajectory Processing
    • Data Processing (Orthophoto, DTM, DSM)
    • Photogrammetry Software: Agisoft Metashape, PIX4D

  • Web & Cloud

    • ArcGIS Online
    • Storymaps
    • ArcGIS Dashboard
    • QGIS2Web
    • Leaflet.js, Folium, MapLibre GL JS

  • Geospatial Intelligence

    • Imagery Intelligence (IMINT)
    • Open Source Intelligence (OSINT)

Download CV

docs.title: Monitoring and Data Program Officer — Global EverGreening Alliance
docs.text:
October 2025 – July 2026 | Melbourne, Australia

  • Assist in the development and implementation of data collection tools for land restoration projects across Africa.
  • Designed and deployed web-based GIS applications and interactive mapping tools to support real-time data collection and project monitoring.
  • Implemented and managed GIS data processing workflows using ArcGIS Pro and other related GIS software
  • Work closely with satellite imagery to assess restoration eligibility in alignment with Gold Standard requirements.

docs.title: GIS Analyst Volunteer — Global EverGreening Alliance
docs.text:
April 2025 – October 2025 | Melbourne, Australia

  • Involved in the Research, Monitoring, Evaluation, and Learning team for carbon and reforestation projects to support GIS tasks pertaining to baseline surveys in Eastern Uganda.
  • Interpret and manage spatial datasets, creating maps, dashboards, and field-ready mapping applications for field data collection using ESRI Environment including ArcGIS Pro, ArcGIS Online, ArcGIS Dashboard, and ArcGIS Field Maps.

docs.title: GIS Analyst — Satu Dental Indonesia
docs.text:
October 2023 – January 2024 | Jakarta, Indonesia

  • Worked closely with cross-functional teams to identify company needs for dental clinic expansion and delivered customized solutions.
  • Conducted spatial data analysis using hexagon and successfully created a dental clinic model for the next expansion area in all over Java and metropolitan cities in Indonesia.
  • Created, updated, and managed geospatial datasets, including mapping the company's competitors and geocoding 50000+ patients to map the distribution of clinic patients.
  • Prepare and deliver reports, presentations, and documentation related to GIS analysis.

docs.title: GIS & Remote Sensing Analyst — PT Garis Posisi Nusantara
docs.text:
October 2023 – January 2024 | Jakarta, Indonesia

  • Worked closely with GIS & Remote Sensing, LIDAR, and photogrammetry for survey and mapping.
  • Conducted complex spatial analysis and remote sensing methodology using advanced GIS/Remote Sensing tools resulting in the production of insightful maps such as Land Use Land Cover Change, DEM, and imagery intelligence.
  • Performed LiDAR and aerial data processing to produce classified point cloud, DTM, DSM, contour, and orthophoto according to client requirements.
  • Collaborated with global imagery vendors about high-resolution satellite imagery products and solutions, prepared and delivered GIS-related training to clients, and worked closely with the field survey and team to ensure quality data.

docs.title: Route Certification Assistant (Freelance) — Jedsy
docs.text:
February 2024 – March 2024 | Switzerland, Switzerland

  • Used QGIS to plan and assess drone flight paths for safe and efficient delivery of medical supplies.
  • Adhered to instructions and detailed guidelines to create accurate drone flight paths.

docs.text:
In [1]: Copied! 
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import folium
import pandas as pd import matplotlib.pyplot as plt import folium In [ ]: Copied! 
# Placeholder time-series data
# Replace with: df = pd.read_csv("your_data.csv")
data = {
    "month": ["Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun",
              "Jul", "Aug", "Sep", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec"],
    "metric_a": [120, 135, 148, 162, 175, 190, 185, 178, 160, 145, 130, 118],
    "metric_b": [ 80,  88,  95, 105, 112, 120, 118, 110, 100,  92,  85,  78],
}

df = pd.DataFrame(data)
df.head()
# Placeholder time-series data # Replace with: df = pd.read_csv("your_data.csv") data = { "month": ["Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun", "Jul", "Aug", "Sep", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec"], "metric_a": [120, 135, 148, 162, 175, 190, 185, 178, 160, 145, 130, 118], "metric_b": [ 80, 88, 95, 105, 112, 120, 118, 110, 100, 92, 85, 78], } df = pd.DataFrame(data) df.head() In [ ]: Copied! 
df[["metric_a", "metric_b"]].describe()
df[["metric_a", "metric_b"]].describe() In [4]: Copied! 
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4))

ax.plot(df["month"], df["metric_a"], marker="o", label="Metric A", color="steelblue")
ax.plot(df["month"], df["metric_b"], marker="s", label="Metric B", color="coral")

ax.set_title("[YOUR CHART TITLE]", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold")
ax.set_xlabel("Month")
ax.set_ylabel("[Unit]")
ax.legend()
ax.grid(axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.5)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4)) ax.plot(df["month"], df["metric_a"], marker="o", label="Metric A", color="steelblue") ax.plot(df["month"], df["metric_b"], marker="s", label="Metric B", color="coral") ax.set_title("[YOUR CHART TITLE]", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold") ax.set_xlabel("Month") ax.set_ylabel("[Unit]") ax.legend() ax.grid(axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.5) plt.tight_layout() plt.show() In [7]: Copied! 
# Placeholder point locations
# Replace with your own lat/lon data, e.g. from a GeoDataFrame or CSV
sites = pd.DataFrame({
    "name":  ["Site A", "Site B", "Site C", "Site D", "Site E"],
    "lat":   [28.61,    28.65,    28.58,    28.70,    28.55],
    "lon":   [77.20,    77.23,    77.17,    77.28,    77.15],
    "value": [142,      168,      95,       210,       87],
})

# Centre the map on the mean location
m = folium.Map(
    location=[sites["lat"].mean(), sites["lon"].mean()],
    zoom_start=11,
    tiles="CartoDB positron",
)

# Add a CircleMarker for each site, scaled by value
for _, row in sites.iterrows():
    folium.CircleMarker(
        location=[row["lat"], row["lon"]],
        radius=row["value"] / 20,          # scale radius to value
        color="steelblue",
        fill=True,
        fill_opacity=0.7,
        tooltip=f"{row['name']}: {row['value']}",
        popup=folium.Popup(
            f"<b>{row['name']}</b><br>Value: {row['value']}",
            max_width=200,
        ),
    ).add_to(m)

m
